programmes.show_description: At Missouri's Crossroads Correctional Facility, Piers comes face-to-face with Lorenzo Gilyard - the Kansas City Strangler.
Not the Right Fit: The final wave of UK recruits arrive. Ben is unsure whether he'll stay in Australia, and Jay has his first day out on the beat and is apprehensive about carrying a weapon.
The Holiday is Over: From first days on the beat to stressful house-hunting, the reality of life in Perth hits our rookie cops. Ben is torn between missing his family and his new amazing life.

The cooks travel to Sydney to meet Peter Gilmore and watch him create squid, scallops and pipis on crispy eggplant with XO sauce - and then recreate the dish from memory.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel to East Sussex in their hunt to discover valuable items 'lost' in people's homes, with their finds including some Victorian street lamps.
The Million Pound Build for Children in Need: The team head to Swansea to build a centre and supported housing for young people in care and leaving care for the Roots Foundation Wales.
Arundel: Nick Knowles meets a West Sussex wife and mother-of-four who was paralysed in a bicycle accident in the summer of 2015, and needs her home adapting for her wheelchair.

Dexter, Driver & Bailey: Noel treats handsome Dobermann Dexter, who has a serious spinal condition, and feisty feline Driver, who's been hit by a car, resulting in hip and knee injuries.
Moon, Milo & Ollie: Eight-year-old Dalmatian Milo's squashed spinal cord causes him constant pain, and Noel attempts to fuse four-year-old Shih Tzu Ollie's elbow in order to save his leg.
Telford: In Shropshire, three schoolchildren, the DIY SOS team and hundreds of volunteers help 12-year-old Matthew return home from hospital for the first time in three years.
The Million Pound Build for Children in Need: The team head to Swansea to build a centre and supported housing for young people in care and leaving care for the Roots Foundation Wales.
A teenager is found with multiple stab wounds in the street near his home, an amateur tree surgeon is badly hurt when a branch falls on his leg and two schoolgirls are run over.
Heart-warming documentary series. Ryan and Keely are teenage sweethearts, while Katie and Steve's baby will need surgery a few hours after birth.
Maternity series set on a buzzing ward in Leeds. Becky and John are naming their baby after their favourite Star Wars character, while Leah's partner will miss the birth.
The broadcaster meets Bronx businessman Alex Henriquez, who was imprisoned for three murders in 1992 but has always protested his innocence.
Confessions of a Serial Killer with Piers Morgan: Piers comes face-to-face with Bernard Giles, who murdered five female hitch-hikers in late 1973.
It's Not All Beaches and Blue Skies: Anna hits the first milestone in Australia without the support of family: Isabelle's 8th birthday. Jay settles into work, but life at home is not so smooth.
Almost Feels Like Home: Maggie and Darren start to settle in at Perth's central station, but Anna struggles with her work-life balance. Michael gets a very unusual reptilian call out.
Shopping from Home.
Today the contestants must keep up with world-renowned chef Curtis Stone to win a 30-minute advantage in the semi-final.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien find a butter churn, a slot machine and a set of trays for storing old printing letters as they continue their mission to turn items into hard cash.
West Bromwich: Nick Knowles and the team, together with West Bromwich volunteers, help a grieving grandmother and her family who are making do in a very small house.
Grenfell Boxing Club: Nick Knowles and the team are constructing two buildings for the Grenfell community, starting with a new home for the Dale Youth Boxing Club.

Betsy & Angel: Cockapoo Betsy was hit by a van five months ago and has developed gangrene, while bite-size bijou Dogue de Bordeaux puppy Angel has a genetic deformity.
Saving Noel's Dog: When Noel's beloved Border Terrier Keira is hit by a van, he must face the possibility that she may not be fixable.
Barnet: Nick Knowles and the DIY SOS team transform the home of a wheelchair-bound police officer who was a victim of the Westminster Bridge terrorist attack in March 2017.

The team treat a lorry driver trapped behind his twisted steering wheel and help is urgently required for a 12-year-old who appears to be drunk after drinking an unknown substance.

programmes.show_name: How To Catch a Serial Killer
programmes.show_description: Former detective Steven Fulcher breaks down the risky investigation that caught infamous murderer Christopher Halliwell - and ended his policing career.
Ghost Ship: When a Bahamas-bound boat is found abandoned 140 miles off course, investigators wade through blood evidence and reports of a possible pirate attack as they look for answers.
Piers meets some of America's most notorious female killers to discover what drives them to kill, starting with Erin Caffey, who masterminded the murder of her family aged 16.
